articles.title: In a drought? Here's how to grow food with less water
articles.summary: In drought-prone regions, watering your vegetable garden is typically allowed even when watering lawns is restricted. But just because you can use water doesn't mean you shouldn't try to conserve it. Here's how you can use a lot less water and still enjoy home-grown produce this year.

articles.title: UK terror threat level raised to 'severe'
articles.summary: The public have been told to "be alert, but not alarmed" after the terror threat level was raised to "severe" following the stabbing of two Jewish men in north London.

articles.summary:
In the hours after the Supreme Court struck down a Louisiana congressional map and weakened protections given to minority voters under the Voting Rights Act, a bishop overseeing historically Black African Methodist Episcopal (AME) churches in Louisiana wrote a message to her district. By limiting how race can be used in drawing congressional districts, the Read more...

articles.title: BREAKING: Louisiana Suspends Primaries After Major Supreme Court Ruling
articles.summary: Louisiana has suspended its May primary elections for congressional races following the Supreme Court ruling on Wednesday on its congressional map, as districts need to be redrawn in order to be legally compliant. The Supreme Court ruled that one of the state’s majority-minority districts was illegally racially gerrymandered. “Yesterday’s historic Supreme Court victory for Louisiana ...

articles.source_summary: Election Law Blog is a U.S.-based legal blog focused on election law, voting rights, and political regulation. It provides commentary, analysis, and updates on court cases, legislation, and academic discussions related to electoral systems, targeting legal professionals, scholars, journalists, and policymakers. The blog is authored by Rick Hasen, a law professor specializing in election law, and operates independently as an academic and commentary platform. Established in 2003, it has become a widely referenced source for developments in U.S. election law and policy. The site publishes frequent posts linking to legal decisions, news articles, and scholarly work. It plays a significant role in informing public debate and academic discourse on voting rights and electoral processes in the United States.

articles.source_summary: The International Centre for Investigative Reporting (ICIR) Nigeria – Investigations & Politics section is part of a nonprofit media organization focused on investigative journalism and public accountability. It provides in-depth reporting on governance, corruption, public policy, and political developments in Nigeria, serving readers interested in detailed analysis and transparency. The organization covers topics such as elections, public finance, and institutional performance, reflecting its focus on accountability journalism. ICIR operates as an independent, nonprofit media outlet supported by grants and partnerships. Founded in 2012, it was established to strengthen investigative reporting and promote transparency in Nigeria’s public sector. Over time, it has developed a reputation for detailed investigations and data-driven journalism. Its role centers on producing in-depth reports and contributing to informed public discourse on governance and political issues.

articles.title: Palestinian FA president refuses to shake hands with Israeli FA vice president at Fifa congress
articles.summary:
Palestinian Football Federation President Jibril Rajoub refused to shake hands and take part in a photo with Israel’s football official Basim Sheikh Suliman during the Fifa Congress in Vancouver on Thursday.

Both men were called to the stand by FIFA President Gianni Infantino, but Rajoub declined to shake hands with Suliman, a Palestinian citizen of Israel.

articles.title: Asesinan a familia en CDMX: detienen a 4 por crimen
articles.summary:
Un caso impactante en la Ciudad de México ha generado conmoción: una familia de cuatro personas fue asesinada dentro de su vivienda en la colonia Nueva Santa María, en la alcaldía Azcapotzalco.

De acuerdo con la Secretaría de Seguridad Ciudadana, las víctimas —una pareja y sus dos hijas de 16 y 12 años— fueron atacadas con arma blanca y presentaban signos de violencia.

Las primeras líneas de investigación apuntaban a un posible intento de extorsión, luego de que se encontrara un mensaje...

articles.title: Activist Omer Aslan says Israeli interception of Gaza flotilla is “unlawful” and “brutal”
articles.summary: A Turkish activist aboard a Gaza-bound aid flotilla has described Israeli intervention in international waters as “unlawful” and “brutal” after several vessels were intercepted overnight. Omer Aslan, speaking from the yacht Girolama, said drones and a helicopter began circling the flotilla before Israeli forces boarded multiple boats, detained around 180 activists and seized at least 21 vessels, according to organisers. Aslan said the remaining boats regrouped off the coast of Crete and...
